taxonomy: Lagarinthus exilis is classified within the family Boraginaceae and order Lamiales. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae, and its taxonomy is further categorized as follows: kingdom Plantae, class Magnoliopsida, order Lamiales, family Boraginaceae, genus Lagarinthus, and species L. exilis. As part of the Boraginaceae family, Lagarinthus exilis shares characteristics with other flowering plants that exhibit similar growth patterns and reproductive structures. The classification of Lagarinthus exilis reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plant species within the same family and order. Understanding the classification of Lagarinthus exilis can provide insights into its adaptations and distribution.
uses: Lagarinthus exilis is a plant species native to tropical Africa.  The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
